dataset.duplicate_rule: Deduplicate within each test set by first occurrence of (source,target); retain an audit warning for 043,045 in test-set0.

design.normalization: For each pair, use one common center and robust diagonal computed from the high-resolution source and target 10th/90th marginal quantiles; apply it unchanged to both resolutions and both directions.
design.randomness: None; full mesh vertices, fixed pair order, deterministic algorithms.

primary_descriptive: absolute high-vs-low symmetric mean surface-fit gap
high-vs-low rigid-translation gap in common dimensionless coordinates
high-vs-low rigid-rotation geodesic gap in degrees
secondary_descriptive: bidirectional rigid-cycle translation and rotation defects
post-alignment symmetric mean, median, and p95 nearest-surface distances
dataset-level Spearman association between match-distance p95 and resolution instability
leave-one-scan-ID-out ranges of medians and paired method differences
endpoints.inferential_tests: None. No p-values or population confidence intervals are permitted because physical specimen identity and independent-unit count are undocumented and scan IDs recur across pairs.

interpretation.raw_coordinates_role: Negative-control fit baseline only. Its identity transform has zero transform and cycle gaps by construction and must not be ranked as a stable registration method on those endpoints.
interpretation.manuscript_rewrite_gate: All 76 unique pairs, four frontends, two resolutions, and two directions must be attempted; failures remain outcomes. A result paragraph may report descriptive robustness or failure evidence after deterministic reproduction and fallacy review.
